Title: The putative endo-1,4-β-D-glucanase GLU3 regulates cellulose biosynthesis in barley roots

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural cell wall polymer required for root growth and integrity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a primary cellulose synthase interacting with HvGLU3 in support of root cellulose biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CESA1 was identified as one of the barley primary CESAs. 2. CESA1 colocalized and directly interacted with HvGLU3 in BiFC assays. 3. Primary CESA genes were reported as downregulated in the elongation zone of hvglu3-1 roots.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic classification; colocalization assay; BiFC assay; RNA-seq expression analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as an experimentally tested interaction partner but not the central causal gene of the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: cellulose biosynthesis / primary cell wall biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: root primary cell wall cellulose biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH9 family endo-1,4-β-glucanase; membrane-anchored KORRIGAN-like protein
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane and punctate compartments surrounding the plasma membrane and nucleus, likely trans-Golgi network-associated compartments
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in the elongation zone versus root cap and meristem in 3-d and 7-d seminal roots; highest among tested tissues in coleoptile; lower in root tip/middle/base and lowest in shoot primordia and youngest leaf zone
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in root elongation zone; broadly expressed in root cap, meristem, and elongation zone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: β-1,4-glucan chains / nascent cellulose glucan chains
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Processed/trimmed β-1,4-glucan chains supporting cellulose microfibril formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putatively hydrolyzes internal β-1,4-glucosidic linkages in glucan chains during cellulose microfibril assembly/editing
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root cell wall, especially seminal root tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major structural component of the plant cell wall; provides tensile strength, cell integrity, and supports root cell elongation and development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cellulose accumulation and proper root cell wall organization; loss of function causes ~60% reduction in root cellulose, short twisted roots, epidermal defects, and altered hemicellulose, lignin, and suberin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HvGLU3 was identified as the causal gene of the short-root barley mutant hvglu3-1 and validated with an independent mutant allele hvglu3-2. 2. HvGLU3 encodes a putative membrane-anchored endo-1,4-β-glucanase required for normal root cellulose biosynthesis. 3. hvglu3 mutants showed a dramatic reduction in root cellulose content together with secondary changes in hemicellulose, lignin, and suberin. 4. HvGLU3 colocalized and directly interacted with primary cellulose synthases CESA1/2/3/5/6, supporting a role in primary cell wall cellulose synthesis. 5. Defective HvGLU3 triggered extensive transcriptomic reprogramming, especially in the elongation zone.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: MutMap+ mapping; allelic validation with independent mutant hvglu3-2; RT-qPCR expression analysis; subcellular localization in Nicotiana benthamiana; isoxaben sensitivity assay; cellulose and cell wall compositional analysis; BiFC interaction assay with CESAs; histological staining; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control component of primary cellulose biosynthesis, but explicit rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream/assembly-associated step in cellulose microfibril biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for proper synthesis/assembly of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Isoxaben treatment; seedling root developmental stage; root tissue context
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing for MutMap+; RNA-seq of laser-capture microdissected root tissues, SRA: PRJNA1120842
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes severely reduced root and shoot growth, epidermal damage, fewer adult tillers, smaller leaf area, reduced seed production, and broad cell wall compositional shifts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: HvGLU3 is highly conserved across barley germplasm with only synonymous coding SNPs detected in 375 accessions; no association with natural root length variation was found. Discussion notes partial functional conservation with rice OsGLU3/KOR1-like proteins and possible role in CSC trafficking.
